children.name: hwTwampResponderTable
children.description: This object indicates the configuration table of TWAMP-light test instances on a reflector.

children.name: hwTwampSenderLossSeqNumberTable
children.description: This object indicates the table for storing the sequence numbers of the latest packet loss sampling intervals.

children.name: hwTwampResetStatisticsTable
children.description: This object indicates resetting the TWAMP-light test session statistics table.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayDelayAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way delay value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampSenderTable
children.description: This object indicates to create a TWAMP-light test instance table.

children.name: hwTwampSenderDelaySeqNumberTable
children.description: This object indicates the table for storing the sequence numbers of the latest delay sampling intervals.

children.name: hwTwampSenderDelaySeqNumberEntry
children.description: This object indicates the entry for storing the sequence number of the latest delay sampling interval.

children.name: hwTwampSenderDelaySeqNumberValue
children.description: This object indicates the sequence number of the latest delay sampling interval.

children.name: hwTwampSenderOperTable
children.description: This object indicates the running status table of a TWAMP-light test instance.

children.name: hwTwampSenderRegularAdminTable
children.description: This object indicates to start a TWAMP-light test instance table.

children.name: hwTwampSenderRegularAdminEntry
children.description: This object indicates to start a TWAMP-light test instance entry.

children.name: hwTwampSenderTwoWayLossTable
children.description: This object indicates a dual-ended packet loss measurement table.

children.name: hwTwampSenderTwoWayLossEntry
children.description: This object indicates a dual-ended packet loss measurement entry.

children.name: hwTwampControllerClientEnable
children.description: The value of this object identifies whether the initiator client is enabled.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayJitterAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way jitter value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayJitterAlarmEntry
children.description: An entry in the table, containing information on the TWAMP two-way jitter alarm entity.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayJitterAlarmRowStatus
children.description: This object indicates the row status of this entry.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayJitterAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way jitter value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayDelayAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way delay value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayDelayAlarmEntry
children.description: An entry in the table, containing information on the TWAMP two-way delay alarm entity.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayDelayValueUpperLimitThreshold
children.description: This object indicates the upper limit(ms) of the two-way ETH-DM.

children.name: hwTwampSenderTwoWayDelayTable
children.description: This object indicates a two-way delay measurement table.

children.name: hwTwampControllerSenderEnable
children.description: The value of this object identifies whether the initiator sender is enabled.

children.name: hwTwampSenderAdminTable
children.description: This object indicates to start a TWAMP-light test instance table.

children.name: hwTwampSenderAdminEntry
children.description: This object indicates to start a TWAMP-light test instance entry.

children.name: hwTwampSenderAdminDuration
children.description: The value of this object identifies the session duration.

children.name: hwTwampSenderAdminContinual
children.description: This object indicates the continuous session.

children.name: hwTwampSenderAdminRowStatus
children.description: This object indicates the row status of this entry.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way loss ratio value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossAlarmEntry
children.description: An entry in the table, containing information on the TWAMP two-way loss alarm entity.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossRatio
children.description: The value of this object identifies the dual-ended packet loss rate.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossRatioLowerLimitThreshold
children.description: The value of this object identifies lower threshold of the dual-ended packet loss rate.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossAlarmRowStatus
children.description: This object indicates the row status of this entry.

children.name: hwTwampTwoWayLossRatioUpperLimitThreshold
children.description: The value of this object identifies upper threshold of the dual-ended packet loss rate.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayLossAlarmTable
children.description: This table is used to query whether the TWAMP two-way loss ratio value triggers alarms.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayLossAlarmEntry
children.description: An entry in the table, containing information on the TWAMP two-way loss alarm entity.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayLossRatio
children.description: The value of this object identifies the dual-ended packet loss rate.

children.name: hwTwampMemTwoWayLossRatioUpperLimitThreshold
children.description: The value of this object identifies upper threshold of the dual-ended packet loss rate.
